evidence_slots.expected_guard: lock registers should be writable only under allowed conditions, become sticky or clear only under authorized reset/unlock, and guard protected write paths at use sites
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: reglk_wrapper gates local AXI acceptance by acct_ctrl_i, and writes to reglk_mem are blocked by selected reglk_ctrl_i bits. However, reset logic uses if (~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9)) to clear all reglk_mem entries to zero, meaning assertion of jtag_unlock clears lock state. The top-level source of jtag_unlock is dmi_jtag, and no additional authorization/stickiness is visible in the reglk_wrapper reset path.

evidence_slots.impact: If jtag_unlock can be asserted by an attacker or unintended debug/test condition, established lock protections can be cleared, permitting later modification of protected control registers and weakening peripheral write protection.

16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
63:         axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
64:         axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
65:         axi_resp_o.b_valid  = 1'b0;
66: 
67:         axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
68:         axi_resp_o.r_valid  = 1'b0;
69: 
70:         address_o      = '0;
71:         we_o           = 1'b0;
72:         en_o           = 1'b0;
73: 
74:         case (state_q)
75:             // we are ready to accept a new request
76:             IDLE: begin
77:                 // we've git a valid write request, we also know that we have asserted the aw_ready
78:                 if (axi_req_i.aw_valid) begin
79:                     axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
80:                     // this costs performance but the interconnect does not obey the AXI standard
81:                     // e.g.: we could wait for aw_valid && w_valid to do the transaction.
82:                     state_d = WRITE;
83:                     // save address
84:                     address_n = axi_req_i.aw
